mission.title: Install and Configure Flowise
mission.description: Flowise is an AI platform that allows users to build AI agents visually. It is ideal for developers looking to create and manage AI workflows without deep technical expertise. Key trade-offs include the need for Node.js and Docker for installation and operation.

steps.title: Install the Flowise CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Flowise command-line interface globally on your machine, allowing you to manage Flowise applications.

```bash

npm install -g flowise@3.0.13

```

> Install the specific version of Flowise globally.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version, node count, and region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create flowise-cluster --num-nodes=3 --zone=us-central1-a --cluster-version=1.30

```

> Ensure you have gcloud CLI installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig file to interact with the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials flowise-cluster --zone=us-central1-a

```

> This allows kubectl to communicate with your cluster.

steps.title: Deploy Flowise on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This step deploys Flowise using a Helm chart, ensuring the application is installed in the correct namespace.

```bash

helm repo add flowise https://flowiseai.github.io/Flowise

helm install flowise flowise/flowise --namespace flowise --create-namespace --version 3.0.13

```

> Add the Flowise Helm repository.
> Install Flowise in the 'flowise' namespace.

steps.title: Verify Flowise Deployment
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Flowise pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n flowise

```

> Look for 'Running' status in the output.

steps.title: Post-Installation Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for the Flowise deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment flowise --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=5 -n flowise

kubectl apply -f monitoring-config.yaml

```

> Set up autoscaling based on CPU usage.
> Apply your monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Flowise
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Flowise deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all Flowise data.

```bash

helm uninstall flowise --namespace flowise

gcloud container clusters delete flowise-cluster --zone=us-central1-a --quiet

```

> This removes the Flowise release.
> This deletes the entire Kubernetes cluster.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Flowise
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This process includes draining nodes if necessary, upgrading the Helm release, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade flowise flowise/flowise --namespace flowise --version 3.0.13

kubectl get pods -n flowise

```

> Drain the node to prepare for upgrade.
> Upgrade to the latest version of Flowise.
> Verify that the Flowise pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image may not exist or there may be network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure your cluster has internet access.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be failing to start due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n flowise' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not be exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service is running and check the service type and port configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added new visual workflow features, improved API performance, and enhanced user interface.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 2.x, review the migration guide for API changes.

resolution.summary: You should see the Flowise UI accessible at http://<your-cluster-ip>:3000 with all services running.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before installation.
